Typical Customer Scenarios

ERP-Centric Manufacturing Organizations

Organizations operating highly integrated manufacturing and supply chain environments often position SAP S/4HANA Sales as the primary platform for Sales Order Management due to tight integration requirements with pricing, ATP, logistics, fulfillment, and financial processes.

Customer Engagement Transformation Programs

Organizations prioritizing sales productivity, customer engagement, mobility, and omnichannel interactions may prefer SAP Sales Cloud solutions integrated with SAP S/4HANA fulfillment capabilities.

Simplification Initiatives

Some organizations intentionally consolidate sales processes into SAP S/4HANA to reduce application landscape complexity, simplify integrations, and centralize operational processes.

Field Sales and Offline Scenarios

Organizations with strong offline mobility requirements may continue leveraging SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C), particularly in scenarios where uninterrupted mobile access remains a critical business requirement.

Conclusion

Sales Order Management remains one of the most strategically important capabilities within the Lead-to-Cash process and continues to play a central role in SAP transformation programs.

As demonstrated throughout this document, SAP S/4HANA Sales remains a strong and valid option for organizations prioritizing operational integration, transactional consistency, advanced pricing, fulfillment orchestration, and simplified ERP-centric architectures. In many industries, especially those with highly operational or logistics-intensive business models, maintaining Sales Order Management directly within SAP S/4HANA can reduce architectural complexity while improving end-to-end process visibility and execution efficiency.

At the same time, organizations should carefully evaluate whether their transformation objectives extend beyond transactional execution into broader customer engagement and sales experience modernization initiatives. In these scenarios, SAP Sales Cloud solutions may provide additional capabilities related to mobility, guided selling, opportunity management, collaboration, AI-assisted sales execution, and omnichannel customer engagement.

Ultimately, the target architecture decision should not be driven solely by product preference, but rather by the organization’s business priorities, business capabilities, operational model, user expectations, integration strategy, and long-term enterprise architecture vision.

There is no universally correct approach for all organizations. The most appropriate solution depends on the balance between operational efficiency, customer engagement requirements, architectural simplification objectives, and transformation strategy.

entry.content:
Hi,

During the printout in forms, when the OPD (Output Determination Parameter) is in the Preparation stage, we have observed that changes made to the material description are reflected in the forms.

I would like to understand:

  • The reason for this behavior, and
  • The recommended solution to control or avoid such changes being reflected.

Regards,
Jagadish

entry.title: Contacting Your SAP Account Executive: A Guide!
entry.content:
Let us introduce the role of the Account Executive and the significant part they play. 

The Account Executive (AE) or Account Owner serves as the essential bridge between SAP and the organization that has entered a contract with SAP. The AE functions as the primary point of contact for the organization, ensuring seamless communication and coordination.  

They are responsible for managing all aspects related to support, product inquiries, retention strategies, and service-related matters. By fostering a strong relationship, the Account Executive ensures that the organization receives comprehensive and personalized assistance, thereby enhancing the overall customer experience. 

The following are examples of topics that can be addressed to your Account Executive:  
• Clarifications on contract verbiage 
• Updates to company address 
• Plans to upgrade software or hardware 
• Access to trial or demo software 
• Questions regarding Service Level Agreements (SLA) 
• Information on education and training events 
• Details on products included with licenses 
• Maintenance renewal inquiries 
• License compliance status 
• Utilization of licenses for compliance 
• Required licenses for the account 
• Information on new purchases/contracts 
• Software usage permissions based on the contract 
• Costs and fees associated with products or services 
• Details about specific license materials (what they are, what they do, etc.) 
• Cloud System/Tenant changes and issues (new cloud products not available yet, etc.) 
• Cloud Installations (deletion, cloud installations not available) 
• Issues with the company's account/contract preventing software downloads and/or generating permanent license keys as indicated by SAP Support 
• Confirmation from the Account Executive is required before submitting a request through CIC for deletion of all Super Administrators if there is no active maintenance linked to the user's account 

Steps to find your AE using the self-service option: 

  1. Navigate to the SAP for Me page (https://me.sap.com/) 
  1. Go to the Users and Contacts Dashboard 
  1. Select Overview  

Users & Contacts - SAP for Me 

For further guidance, please refer to the Knowledge Base Article on how to contact the Account Executive: SAP Note: 2626344 

If you are still unable to connect with the Account Executive, the Customer Interaction Center  is always available to assist you by providing the Account Executive's information from SAP to your organization.  

The Customer Interaction Center can provide the name, email address, and phone number of the Account Executive (AE) or account owner by requesting the Customer ERP number or installation number, the email address with the same domain ID, and the S-User ID. 

https://support.sap.com/en/contact-us.html?anchorId=section 

 Thanks for reading! 

 We hope you found this helpful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have! 

Read more Customer Interaction Center blog posts Here   

If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Issue on using System User for Scheduled CBTA execution
entry.content:
The user has to be of type "Dialog" to be able to logon to the SUT. System user will not work.

Please see the documentation in the system SU01 - move to mouse to Field "User Type" and hit F1.

System 'B'

Use the system user type for internal system processes (-> background processing) or system-related processes (-> ALE, workflow, TMS, CUA).

Dialog logon (using SAP GUI) is not possible.

A user of this type is excluded from the general settings for password validity. Only user administrators can change the password using transaction SU01 (Goto -> Change Password).

entry.title: Re: EDI in the age of agentic AI, the structural mismatch nobody is naming
entry.content:
Thank you, Egbert.

On maturity, SAP Business Network is proven and widely adopted. Now 29+ years old (established in 1997), it is the largest and most mature network of its kind in the industry, with asset and logistics processes added more recently in 2016 and 2018. Thousands of customers, including almost all of the world’s largest companies, use it to collaborate with their supply chain in some form.

IDC recently (2024) conducted an analysis of both the buy and sell sides. The results were compelling:

  • Buy side: 404% ROI, 30% faster go-to-market, 27% faster product/order delivery time
  • Sell side: 416% ROI, 20% faster go-to-market, 50% faster time to invoice

As a smart follower, this is mature and well beyond early-adopter territory.

entry.title: Re: 在采购订单表头新增自定义字段失败
entry.content:
Good day @caoka 

Thank you for your question.

Based on the translation and the lack of exact screenshots that display the error you face, I can say the following.

Achieving that in Public Cloud will depend on the field being created under the correct business context and the required usages being enabled.

For your scenario, because the purchase order is created via interface and the coding/process value must be returned to SAP, you should check whether the custom field was enabled for the relevant purchase order UI/API usage. See KBA 3034002 for more details on enabling purchase order custom fields.

In simple terms:

  • Creating the custom field is not enough.
  • You must create is in the correct Purchases Order Header business context.
  • The required usage must be enabled, for example, UI, API, form template, or reporting.
  • If the purchase order is created or updated via interface, the interface must support the custom field.
  • If the custom field fails when added, check whether the field type, usage, or business context is supported for purchase order header.
  • Field control for purchase order custom fields is controlled through extensibility/BAdIs, not through classic SSCUI field selection. See KBA 3268822 for more details.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: ATUL Architecture #3: Bridging OT and IT: Delivering Mission-Critical Grid Data to SAP BTP Safely, S
entry.content:
ATUL Architecture #3: Bridging OT and IT: Delivering Mission-Critical Grid Data to SAP BTP Safely, Scalably, and Cleanly

1.      Introduction

Utilities are accelerating their digital transformation, but one architectural frontier remains notoriously difficult to conquer:

How do you safely ingest high-frequency Operational Technology (OT) data from substations, feeders, and Intelligent Electronic Devices (IEDs) into SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) without introducing cyber vulnerabilities or disrupting grid reliability?

Legacy architecture often relied on brittle, custom-coded point-to-point connections or heavy middleware that violated basic security isolation principles. This article provides a comprehensive, production-ready, architecture-driven blueprint for securely integrating:

  • IEC 61850 substation telemetry (GOOSE/MMS)
  • MQTT/Sparkplug B telemetry streams
  • SCADA / RTU / IED asynchronous events
  • Grid-edge IoT sensors

We will achieve this by leverage modern event-driven architectures (EDA), Zero-Trust Network Access (ZTNA), and SAP Clean Core design patterns.

2.      Why OT-to-IT Integration Challenges Enterprise

Bridging the physical grid and the digital cloud requires reconciling two fundamentally opposing engineering philosophies:

Dimension

Operational Technology (OT)

Enterprise Information Technology (IT)

Primary Priority

Availability & Safety: Human life and grid stability. Zero downtime tolerated.

Confidentiality & Integrity: Data security, compliance, and financial accuracy.

Protocols

Industrial, low-overhead, non-IP, or deterministic (IEC 61850, DNP3, Modbus).

Web-native, human-readable, RESTful (HTTPS, OData, gRPC).

Network Philosophy

Air-gapped, strictly segmented (Purdue Model Level 0-3), highly localized.

Cloud-native, perimeterless, distributed, global access.

Data Characteristics

Ultra-high frequency (milliseconds), time-series bursts, massive velocity.

Transactional, structured, stateful, request-response.

To bridge this gap safely, cloud ingestion must be completely outbound-only from the lower levels, strictly audited, cryptographically verified, and decoupled via an enterprise event broker.

3.      Business impact on Utilities

This architecture is not just a technical modernization—it directly enables:

  • Reduced unplanned outages through real-time anomaly detection
  • Lower maintenance costs via predictive asset health insights
  • Improved regulatory compliance with secure audit-ready telemetry flows
  • Faster field response through automated S/4HANA work order generation
  • Scalable grid-edge onboarding without re-platforming legacy OT systems

4.      Reference Architecture: Purdue Model to SAP BTP

The architecture below maps the telemetry flow across the classic Purdue Model for Industrial Control Systems, ensuring that no direct connection is ever established between the cloud and Level 2/3 critical infrastructure.

5.      Architectural Safeguards Highlighted:

  • Directional Isolation: Firewalls block all inbound traffic to the Substation OT network. The Industrial Edge Gateway establishes an outbound connection to the Upper DMZ broker.
  • Decoupling Layer: SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) acts as the shock absorber, ensuring that high-frequency grid telemetry bursts do not overwhelm downstream ERP APIs.

*Architectural Principle: Direct OT-to-cloud integrations without DMZ brokers are inherently unsafe.  Any architecture that bypasses the industrial DMZ violates fundamental Purdue Model isolation and must be avoided.

6.      Deep-Dive: Protocol Translation & Topic Design

 The Conversion: IEC 61850 to Lightweight Cloud Events: IEC 61850 uses Manufacturing Message Specification (MMS) for complex reporting and Generic Object-Oriented Substation Events (GOOSE) for peer-to-peer microsecond trips. These cannot be parsed by cloud apps directly.

An Edge Gateway maps these complex hierarchical Logical Nodes (e.g., MMXU1 for Three-Phase Voltage/Current) into standard JSON structures wrapped in the CloudEvents specification.

Production-Grade Ingestion Payload Example

Below is the exact schema passed from the Upper DMZ to SAP Advanced Event Mesh when a transformer experiences a thermal anomaly:

JSON { "specversion": "1.0",

  "id": "evt-773a-4b92-88ef-22114949ad45", "source": "/us-east/substation-s12/transformer-tx03",   "type":

"com.utility.grid.telemetry.v1",

  "datacontenttype": "application/json",

  "time": "2026-06-01T18:21:55.003Z",

  "data": { "logical_node": "YPTR1",

    "component": "WindingTemperature",

    "metrics": {  "temperature_celsius": 98.4,

      "ambient_celsius": 32.1,

      "cooling_fan_status": "ON_MAX" },

    "status": { "health_score": 72.5,

      "anomaly_detected": true,

      "severity": "WARNING" } } }

Enterprise Topic Hierarchy Best Practices

To optimize routing efficiency within SAP Advanced Event Mesh and avoid bloated subscription wildcards, we enforce a structured, predictable topic hierarchy layout:

$$\text{Domain} \,/\, \text{Business Unit} \,/\, \text{Geographic Zone} \,/\, \text{Substation ID} \,/\, \text{Asset Category} \,/\, \text{Asset ID} \,/\, \text{Event Type}$$

Examples:

  • grid/distribution/northeast/sub-s12/transformer/tx03/telemetry
  • grid/transmission/midwest/sub-s45/circuit-breaker/cb07/state

This allows BTP consumers to subscribe with surgical precision. For instance, a predictive maintenance engine can listen to all transformer telemetry across the entire Northeast region using the pattern: grid/distribution/northeast/+/transformer/+/telemetry.

7.      The Blueprint in Action: A Practitioner Scenario

To understand how this architecture operates under pressure, let's trace a real-world grid event: A severe thermal anomaly on Substation Transformer TX03 located in the Northeast distribution zone.

Step 1: The Edge Trigger (Purdue Level 2)

  1. The physical temperature sensor on Transformer TX03 detects a sudden spike, hitting $98.4^\circ\text{C}$.
  2. The local Intelligent Electronic Device (IED) broadcasts this telemetry via an IEC 61850 MMS (Manufacturing Message Specification) reporting block over the substation's internal fiber optic bus.

Step 2: Protocol Translation & Hardening (Industrial DMZ Level 3.5)

  1. The OT Industrial Edge Gateway (running Litmus Edge) intercepts the raw MMS packet.
  2. It strips out the heavy industrial headers, extracts the data fields from the Logical Node YPTR1 (the standard IEC 61850 node for a power transformer), and maps them into a standardized, web-friendly JSON payload.
  3. The gateway packages this payload inside a standardized CloudEvents v1.0 wrapper.

Step 3: Secure Egress to the DMZ Proxy (Enterprise DMZ Level 3.5)

  1. The Edge Gateway initiates an outbound-only TLS 1.3 connection to the Upper DMZ MQTT Broker (HiveMQ Cluster).
  2. It passes hardware-secured mTLS client certificates to prove its identity and publishes the message to the strict, isolated topic:

 “grid/distribution/northeast/sub-s12/transformer/tx03/telemetry”

Step 4: Ingestion by SAP Advanced Event Mesh (SAP BTP Layer)

  1. The HiveMQ cluster securely routes the incoming stream over a secure WebSocket connection into SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) via Dynamic Message Routing (DMR).
  2. Because AEM natively understands the hierarchical topic layout, it instantly clones and routes the message to three concurrent, decoupled subscribers without any database lag:
    • SAP Datasphere Ingestion Queue: For long-term historical time-series storage and grid analytics.
    • SAP AI Core Engine: To recalculate the transformer's Remaining Useful Life (RUL) score.
    • CAP Event Processor Queue: Dedicated to immediate, critical rule evaluation.

Step 5: Real-Time Orchestration & Clean Core Action

  1. A lightweight SAP CAP (Cloud Application Model) microservice consumes the message from its dedicated AEM queue.
  2. The service executes an automated rule: If temperature_celsius > $95^\circ\text{C}$ and cooling_fan_status == "ON_MAX", trigger emergency field intervention.
  3. Rather than writing complex custom ABAP tables inside the ERP, the CAP service uses the standard SAP Integration Suite to call the out-of-the-box S/4HANA OData v4 API (API_EQUIPMENT_SRV).
  4. The Result: An automated Urgent Corrective Maintenance Work Order is instantly generated in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management with a high-priority dispatch flag—all accomplished within 2.3 seconds of the physical temperature spike, leaving the digital core clean, secure, and upgrade-ready.

8.      Advanced Substation-to-BTP Integration Patterns

 *Reality Check*: Batch ingestion architectures are fundamentally incompatible with modern grid telemetry. Millisecond-level data streams require real-time, event-driven processing models—not delayed batch pipelines.

Depending on your edge compute availability and governance requirements, choose one of these production deployment topologies:

Pattern A: Multi-Tiered Message Broker Architecture (Recommended for Zero-Trust)

  • Mechanism: An Edge Gateway pushes local data to an on-premises DMZ MQTT cluster (e.g., Hive MQ). Secure bridge configuration clones and forwards permitted topics out to SAP Advanced Event Mesh (AEM) via cloud connectors or secure WebSocket’s.
  • Pros: Strict isolation; if the cloud connection drops, the local DMZ broker caches events, mitigating data loss.

Pattern B: Intelligent Edge Compute with Stream Processing (Recommended for High Frequency)

  • Mechanism: Deploy an industrial edge compute node (running lightweight runtimes like Node-RED or KubeEdge) physically inside the substation. It aggregates millisecond telemetry, calculates rolling 1-minute means/standard deviations, and publishes only summarized statistical health events to SAP BTP.
  • Pros: Radically reduces egress data costs and avoids overwhelming BTP with trivial data points.

9.      Security & Governance Blueprint (Non-Negotiable Controls)

Bringing OT data to the cloud is impossible without an airtight security architecture. Implement the following parameters at each layer:

  • Mutual TLS (mTLS) with Hardware Roots of Trust: Every edge gateway must authenticate against the DMZ broker using X.509 client certificates issued by an internal Utility Certificate Authority (CA). Private keys must be stored in secure TPM 2.0 hardware chips on the edge device.
  • Granular Access Control Lists (ACLs): Edge gateways must be explicitly barred from publishing to topics outside their assigned asset scope. A compromise of Substation A must never allow injection of malicious events into Substation B.
  • Schema Validation at the Ingress Gate: Use the Schema Validation features in SAP Advanced Event Mesh. If a message arrives containing bad structures, malformed types, or executable code payloads, it is immediately shunted to a Dead Letter Queue (DLQ) for forensics, blocking code injection vectors.
  • Network Partitioning & Backpressure Controls: If the connection between the utility DMZ and SAP BTP degrades, the DMZ broker must utilize Local Storage Buffering. High QoS (Quality of Service) 1 or 2 settings must be applied strictly to critical operational state changes (e.g., breaker trips) to guarantee delivery, while standard continuous metrics use QoS 0 to save bandwidth.

10. Realizing Enterprise Value in SAP BTP

Once normalized grid events stream cleanly into SAP Advanced Event Mesh, they become actionable assets without touching the digital core directly:

*Strategic Insight*: Event Mesh is not optional—it is the control plane for grid-scale ingestion.  Without event-driven decoupling, high-frequency OT telemetry cannot be safely or scalable integrated into enterprise systems.

The Event Consumer Microservices Layer

Deploy lightweight, stateless microservices within the SAP BTP Kyma Runtime or SAP Cloud Application Model (CAP) to process incoming streams:

 

 

 

  • Real-Time Grid Insights: Kyma functions scan the event properties. If a transformer temperature exceeds defined thresholds (temperature_celsius > 95), an immediate event is triggered.
  • AI-Driven Asset Health Optimization: Data streams scale out directly into SAP Datasphere and SAP AI Core. Machine Learning models calculate remaining useful life (RUL) metrics based on historical load/thermal characteristics, sending updated health indices back to operational dashboards.

The Clean Core Imperative

By processing events asynchronously in BTP, the core enterprise system (SAP S/4HANA / IS-U) remains entirely untouched by heavy custom code. S/4HANA only participates when action is required—such as creating an asset maintenance order or executing a field service dispatch via standard standard OData v4 APIs. The system updates and upgrades effortlessly, unencumbered by massive custom databases full of RAW time-series data.

11. End-to-End Flow: Transformer Overheating Scenario

  • A transformer in Substation S12 exceeds thermal threshold (98.4°C).
  • IEC 61850 MMS reports are captured by Edge Gateway.
  • Gateway converts payload to Cloud Events JSON.
  • Event is securely published (mTLS) to DMZ MQTT broker.
  • Broker forwards allowed topics to SAP Advanced Event Mesh.
  • AEM routes the event to a CAP microservice.
  • CAP evaluates rules → triggers anomaly event.
  • SAP AI Core enriches with predictive health score.
  • SAP S/4HANA automatically creates a maintenance work order.
  • Field service team is dispatched proactively.

12. Operational Summary Blueprint

  1. The convergence of OT and IT is no longer optional—it is foundational to modern utilities.
  2. However, success depends not on connectivity alone, but on *how* that connectivity is governed. 
  3. Architectures that prioritize strict isolation, event-driven decoupling, and Clean Core principles will define the next generation of grid intelligence.
  4. SAP BTP, when used correctly, becomes not just an integration platform—but the control tower for real-time, autonomous utility operations.

entry.title: Re: Additional “Attributes” button is displayed in CJ8VH...
entry.content:
Hello, 

The additional "Attributes" button appearing in the post-upgrade environment is a standard SAP variant management feature. It allows users to maintain variant properties such as user-specific settings, default flags, and field visibility.

This is a UI enhancement delivered in the upgraded system version and has no functional impact on existing variants or processes. May I know if you face any error or difficulties?
